member.name: Paulette Hamilton
member.synopsis: Paulette Hamilton is the Labour MP for Birmingham Erdington , and has been an MP continually since 3 March 2022.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Brain: Tumours
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what data his Department holds on the fresh freezing and storage of brain tumour tissue, including data collected through NHS England’s gap analysis of freezer capacity; and if he will provide the
writtenQuestions.answerText: NHS England has completed a gap analysis of the need for freezer capacity and is looking at how to support services to provide an equitable expansion of that capacity. The Department and NHS England will work with professional bodies, including the Roya...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Employment: Discrimination
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for Women and Equalities, what assessment she has made of the potential impact of hair-based discrimination on women’s employment outcomes, particularly for Black and mixed-heritage women; and whether she plans to tackle that issue in
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the Hon Member to the answer on 12 June 2026 (PQ UIN 6577).

writtenQuestions.heading: Discrimination
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for Women and Equalities, what consideration she has given to updating equality legislation to help tackle hair-based discrimination.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The government wants to ensure that whoever you are, wherever you come from, Britain is a country that will respect your contribution and give you a fair chance to get on in life. The Equality Act 2010 uses a very broad definition of race, so that it prot...

writtenQuestions.heading: Pupils: Ethnic Groups
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what guidance her Department provides on preventing unwanted touching of (a) Black and (b) mixed-heritage children’s Afro-textured hair in (i) early years and (ii) school settings; how this issue is addressed w
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer my hon. Friend, the Member for Birmingham Erdington to the answer of 10 June 2026 to Question 6581.

writtenQuestions.heading: Personal Care Services: Qualifications
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what assessment her Department has made of the equalities impact of (a) not routinely including Afro-textured hair within hairdressing qualifications and (b) individuals having been denied access to hairdressin
writtenQuestions.answerText: We are committed to improving the quality of hair and beauty qualifications and have plans in place to reform all qualifications for 16 to 19 learners. Content for any new hair and beauty qualifications, including new Level 2 Occupational Certificates and...

writtenQuestions.heading: Pupils: Discrimination
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, whether training on (a) race, (b) consent, and (c) safeguarding in relation to (i) children’s hair, (ii) Afro hair and (iii) bodily autonomy forms part of (A) early years and (B) teacher training requirements;
writtenQuestions.answerText: The department is committed to ensuring that all children receive a high-quality and inclusive education, supported by well-trained early years practitioners and teachers.To achieve qualified teacher status and early years teacher status, trainees must me...

writtenQuestions.heading: Caribbean: Development Aid

writtenQuestions.heading: British Council: Commonwealth
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what role the British Council plays in delivering the UK’s International Education Strategy in Commonwealth countries.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The ambitions in the UK’s International Education Strategy will be delivered in close collaboration between government, the sector, the UK’s International Education Champion and key partners including the British Council. The reformed Education Sector Act...

writtenQuestions.heading: Health Services: Infrastructure and Planning Permission
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, if he will conduct a review of local planning and healthcare infrastructure for villages and local hub–type plans, including PFI estates.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Planning regulation and approval is a matter for the Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government. The National Health Service operates in accordance with published planning guidance.However, we recognise delivering high-quality NHS healthcare se...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Pharmacy: Sustainable Development
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps he is taking to help support the sustainability of NHS pharmacies operating on PFI sites.
writtenQuestions.answerText: For 2025/26, funding for the core community pharmacy contractual framework was increased to £3.1 billion. This represented the largest uplift in funding of any part of the National Health Service at the time, over 19% across 2024/25 and 2025/26. As is cus...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Pharmacy: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, if he will consider NHS funding adjustments for pharmacies to offset increases in National Minimum Wage and employer National Insurance.
writtenQuestions.answerText: For 2025/26, funding for the core community pharmacy contractual framework was increased to £3.1 billion. This represented the largest uplift in funding of any part of the National Health Service at the time, over 19% across 2024/25 and 2025/26. As is cus...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Pharmacy: Private Finance Initiative
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, if he will review financial relief for pharmacies on PFI estates, including rent-subsidy schemes and a statutory cap on PFI rents for NHS pharmacy contractors.
writtenQuestions.answerText: For 2025/26, funding for the core community pharmacy contractual framework was increased to £3.1 billion. This represented the largest uplift in funding of any part of the National Health Service at the time, over 19% across 2024/25 and 2025/26. As is cus...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Buildings: Climate Change
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what steps he is taking to help ensure that new buildings include (a) insulation, (b) green roofs, (c) passive cooling and (d) other climate-related features through his Departme
writtenQuestions.answerText: Through the Building Regulations, the Department sets minimum performance standards for new homes and buildings. In 2021, these standards were strengthened to ensure new homes and buildings are highly energy-efficient, with high-quality insulation and eff...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Climate Change
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what steps she is taking with Cabinet colleagues to assess the potential impact of climate-related risks to (a) health, (b) infrastructure and (c) the economy on the Government's (i) p
writtenQuestions.answerText: It is the Government’s statutory duty to assess climate risks to the UK every five years under the Climate Change Act, a process known as the UK Climate Change Risk Assessment (CCRA), followed by a National Adaptation Programme (NAP), setting out actions ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Social Security Benefits: Palliative Care
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, if he will make an assessment of the potential merits of reviewing the effectiveness of the Special Rules for End of Life fast-tracking benefits system.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The primary way the Department supports people nearing the end of life is through special benefit rules which are known as the Special Rules for End of Life (SREL). These enable people who are nearing the end of their lives to get faster, easier access to...

writtenQuestions.heading: Palliative Care: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, what steps he is taking to help improve the financial security of people at the end of life.
writtenQuestions.answerText: This Government is committed to providing a financial safety net for those who need it. The primary way the Department supports people nearing the end of life is through special benefit rules which are known as the Special Rules for End of Life (SREL). Th...

writtenQuestions.heading: Terminal Illnesses: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, if he will make an assessment of the potential implications for his policies of the recommendations of the report by the All Party Parliamentary Group on Hospice and End of Life Care entitled Inquiry in
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department supports people nearing the end of life through the Special Rules for End of Life. These enable people who are nearing the end of their lives to get faster, easier access to certain benefits, without needing to attend a medical assessment o...

writtenQuestions.heading: State Retirement Pensions: Terminal Illnesses
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, if he will take steps to expand the scope of the terms of reference for the Pensions Commission to enable it to consider the potential merits of allowing people of working age with a terminal illness to
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Terms of Reference for the Pensions Commission, which set out the scope for the Commission, were published on the 21st July. As set out in their Terms of Reference, the Commissioners will consider what is required in the long term to deliver financial...

writtenQuestions.heading: Maternity Pay
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, if she will award maternity pay per baby instead of per pregnancy.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We want new mothers to be able to take time away from work in the later stages of their pregnancy and in the months following childbirth, in the interests of their own and their baby’s health and wellbeing. Maternity pay is paid for each pregnancy, not in...
